_doc: Shared path-containment contract between the Python core (forge/context) and the TypeScript opencode plugin. Both test suites load this file and substitute @REPO@ with a real git repo, @OUTSIDE@ with a sibling temp dir, @SCRATCH@ with a scratch temp dir, and @HANDLE@ with a real stored result handle. CI fails on both sides if either diverges.

description: normal relative path stays inside the repository
deeply nested relative path stays inside the repository
absolute path that resolves inside the repository is allowed
absolute path pointing outside the repository is unsafe
dotdot that collapses back inside the repository is allowed
dotdot that escapes the repository root is unsafe
dotdot from a subdirectory that escapes the repository root is unsafe
path through a symlink whose target is inside the repository is allowed
path through a symlink pointing outside the repository is unsafe even when the leaf does not exist
path through a symlink pointing outside the repository is unsafe when the leaf exists
path with a not-yet-created intermediate directory inside the repository is allowed
path containing an embedded null byte is unsafe
note: Python Path.resolve raises ValueError (not OSError) for embedded null bytes; the harness treats any exception as unsafe. TS catches the exception in visit (D4 fix) and treats it as unsafe.

description: empty path string resolves to the repository root and is allowed
path key nested inside a dict is still inspected
path keys inside a list are inspected; any escaping member is unsafe
filepath key is recognised as a path key on both sides (D1 fix)
note: D1: Python governor previously did not include filepath in path_keys; TS already did.

description: non-path key with an escaping value is ignored
backslash is a literal character on posix so the path stays inside
backslash is a path separator on win32 so the path escapes
safe_path rejects all absolute paths even when they are inside the repo
note: safe_path is stricter than the governor: it never accepts absolute paths.

description: safe_path rejects dotdot that escapes the repository
safe_path accepts a normal relative path
handle containing dotdot is malformed and rejected
handle starting with a slash is malformed and rejected
handle containing a null byte is malformed and rejected
raw file replaced by a symlink pointing outside storage is rejected (D9 fix)
note: D9: harness stores a real result, obtains the handle, then replaces the .raw file with a symlink to @OUTSIDE@/secret.txt. Both sides must reject.
